memoryArtifacts.headline: Edmund Rice Homesite Marker
memoryArtifacts.description: Edmund Rice Homesite Marker designed by Boston architect Arthur Wallace Rice located in the current town of Wayland (formerly East Sudbury), Massachusetts at global coordinates 42.343814°N 71.365357°W near the "Old Connecticut Path" Indian Trail.[1] Although the marker raised in 1913 states that Edmund Rice was born in Buckinghamshire, England, later research in the 1930s found that he was most likely to have been born in Suffolk.

zh.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice於大約1594年出生在英國素福爾可斯坦斯亞德。他於1618年10月15日與Thomasine Frost在英國英格蘭素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲聖 馬也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1638年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素德波利。他於1648年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利被列為deacon in the puritan church。他去世於1663年5月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾波洛享年70歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可瓦易藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice于大约1594年出生在英国素福爾可斯坦斯亞德。他于1618年10月15日与Thomasine Frost在英国英格兰素福爾可波利 聖 艾德蒙茲聖 馬也斯 邱奇结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1638年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素德波利。他于1648年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可素德波利被列为deacon in the puritan church。他去世于1663年5月3日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可馬爾波洛享年70岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州米德萊可瓦易藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Rice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: Anglicized pronunciation of one of the most common Welsh personal names, Rhys, from a form originally meaning ‘rash, impetuous’, also spelled Rys and Re(e)s. See also Reese , with which it is interchangeable as a result of different Anglicized forms of the Welsh vowel y, and also compare Preece and Price . Initial R- in Welsh is voiceless and often spelled Rh-, but in English R- is voiced as in the Anglicized surnames Rees and Rice. Welsh y is a short back vowel /ɪ/. In the medieval period the English approximation of this vowel was either /i/ or /e/, lengthened to /i:/ and /e:/. Subsequent sound changes in English produced the alternative pronunciations represented in Rees, Preece and Rice, Price. The name has also been established in Ireland from an early date.

English: either a topographic name for someone who lived in or near a thicket (Middle English ris, rice, ris, from Old English hrīs, Old Norse hrís), or a habitational name for someone who came from a place called with this word, such as Rise (East Yorkshire).

English: perhaps a nickname from Middle English Rys(e) and Re(e)s which when without a preposition could derive from one or other of several Old French and Middle English words, including Anglo-Norman French ris ‘laughter, smile’, Middle English ris, res ‘stem, stalk’, in origin the same word as in 2 above, and Middle English ris, rise, rice, res, Old French ris, riz ‘rice’, perhaps a nickname for a rice dealer or a cook.

variants.name: Reese
meanings: North German and Dutch (De Reese): nickname for a very big man, from Middle Low German, Middle Dutch rese ‘giant’.
German: habitational name from any of the places called Rees or Reese, in Rhineland and Lower Saxony.

variants.name: Rees
meanings: German: habitational name from a place in the Rhineland called Rees.
German: from a short form of the personal name   Andreas  .

variants.name: Ryce
meanings: Welsh and English: variant of   Rice  .

variants.name: Rise
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Aase, Lief, Sven.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of over twenty farmsteads named Rise, from Old Norse hrís ‘brushwood’. The name also occurs in Sweden and Denmark.

variants.name: Wrice
meanings: Probably Welsh or English: variant of   Rice  .

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice was born about 1594, in Stanstead, Suffolk, England. He married Thomasine Frost on 15 October 1618, in St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. He immigrated to Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America in 1638. In 1648, at the age of 55, his occupation is listed as deacon in the puritan church in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. He died on 3 May 1663, in Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 70, and was buried in Wayland, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice est né environ 1594, à Stanstead, Suffolk, Angleterre. Il a épousé Thomasine Frost le 15 octobre 1618, à St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Il a immigré au Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1638. En 1648, à l’âge de 55 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est deacon in the puritan church à Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Il est décédé le 3 mai 1663, à Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Wayland, Middlesex, Massachusetts, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice wurde um 1594 in Stanstead, Suffolk, England geboren. Er hat am 15. Oktober 1618 in St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom Thomasine Frost geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er ist 1638 nach Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America eingewandert. 1648, als er 55 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als deacon in the puritan church in Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America aufgeführt. Er starb am 3. Mai 1663 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Wayland, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice è nato il circa 1594 nel Stanstead, Suffolk, England. Lui sposato Thomasine Frost il 15 ottobre 1618, nel St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Immigrato nel Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America nel 1638. Nel 1648, all'età di 55 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come deacon in the puritan church nel Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Morì il 3 maggio 1663, nel Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Wayland, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Riceは約1594年にイギリススフォルクスタンステアドで生まれた。1618年10月15日にイギリスイギリススフォルクブリー スト エドムンズスト マリ'ス チャーチでThomasine Frostと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1638年にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースドブリーに移住した。1648年，55歳の時，イギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックススドブリーでの職業はdeacon in the puritan churchと記録されている。彼は1663年5月3日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスマルルボロウで70歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスワイランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice(은)는 대략 1594년 영국 수폴크 스탠스테아드에서 태어났다. 그는 1618년 10월 15일 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 버이 스트 에드먼드즈 스트 마이'스 c후르치에서 Thomasine Frost(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수드버이로 이민을 떠났다. 1648년, 55세의 나이로 그의 직업은 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 수드버이에서 deacon in the puritan church이었다. 그는 1663년 5월 3일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 말를버루그h에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 웨이랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice nasceu aproximadamente 1594, em Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Ele casou-se com Thomasine Frost em 15 de outubro de 1618, em St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele imigrou para o Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America em 1638. Em 1648, com 55 anos, sua profissão aparece como deacon in the puritan church em Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Ele faleceu em 3 de maio de 1663, em Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Wayland, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice родился около 1594 г. в местности станстид, суффолк, Англия. Он женился на женщине по имени Thomasine Frost 15 октября 1618 г. в местности ст мэри'с чурч, бури ст эдмундс, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он иммигрировал в судбури, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1638 году. В 1648 году, в возрасте 55 лет,  он работал по профессии deacon in the puritan church в местности судбури, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. Он умер 3 мая 1663 г. в местности марлбороую, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности веиланд, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Deacon Edmund Rice nació aproximadamente 1594, en Stanstead, Suffolk, Inglaterra. Se casó con Thomasine Frost el 15 de octubre de 1618, en St Mary's Church, Bury St Edmunds, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Inmigró a Sudbury, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America en 1638. En 1648, a la edad de 55 años, su ocupación registrada era deacon in the puritan church en Sudbury, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Murió el 3 de mayo de 1663, en Marlborough, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Wayland, Middlesex, Masachusets, Estados Unidos.
